source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 使激动; 使混乱 ， 使不安 to stir up; disturb, disorder: changes that have roiled the country 使国家陷入动乱的变化 v.i. (液体 ) 搅动;翻滚;翻腾 (of a liquid) move in a turbulent, swirling manner: the roiling flood water 翻滚着的洪水

headline.core_en: to make liquid muddy or turbulent; to disturb or upset

familiar_bridge.summary: roil 的画面像水被搅得翻腾浑浊；引申到局势或情绪，就是被搅乱。

core.main: roil 可以说水流翻腾，也可以说市场、政治、情绪被扰乱。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 水或液体被搅动后不平静、不清澈。

examples.sentence: The storm roiled the harbor waters.
examples.translation: 风暴搅得港湾水面翻腾。
examples.teaching_note: roil water 是物理画面。

usage_groups.group_title: 局势、市场或情绪被扰乱
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 抽象语境中表示本来稳定的状态被搅乱。

examples.sentence: The unexpected announcement roiled financial markets.
examples.translation: 这项意外公告搅动了金融市场。
examples.teaching_note: roil markets 是新闻中常见用法。

examples.sentence: The community was roiled by accusations of corruption.
examples.translation: 腐败指控让这个社区陷入动荡。
examples.teaching_note: be roiled by 表示被某事扰乱。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The speech stirred up anger.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: stir up 更普通；roil 更有翻腾动荡感。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The noise disturbed the meeting.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: disturb 更宽泛。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The news agitated the crowd.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: agitate 可指液体或情绪被搅动。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The scandal convulsed the government.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: convulse 程度更剧烈。

antonyms.example_sentence: The speech helped calm the crowd.
antonyms.difference_note: calm 与 roil 的扰乱相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The dust settled after the trucks passed.
antonyms.difference_note: settle 可指液体/局势恢复平静。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The water began to boil.
confusable_words.difference_note: boil 是因热沸腾；roil 是翻腾搅乱。

word_family.example_sentence: Roiling clouds gathered over the hills.

related_words.note: 与 roil 的翻腾动荡义相关。
related_words.example_sentence: The country entered a turbulent period.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt. FRAUD; 激烈批评 to attack violently with blows or words: The movie was assailed by critics. 电影遭到了评论家的口诛笔伐 。 派生 unassailable /,ano'seilabl/ adj. 不可反驳的; 无懈可击的 unable to be attacked, questioned, or defeated: an unassailable alibi 无可质疑的不在犯罪现场的申辩近义词 sound

headline.core_cn: 猛烈攻击；严厉批评；困扰
headline.core_en: to attack strongly, physically or verbally; to trouble intensely

familiar_bridge.summary: assault 是攻击；assail 更书面，可指实际攻击，也可指语言猛烈抨击。

core.main: assail 常见于批评、攻击、质疑，也可说人被恐惧、疑虑等困扰。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调攻击力度强，可以是身体攻击，也可以是言辞攻击。

examples.sentence: Opponents assailed the policy as unfair and costly.
examples.translation: 反对者猛烈批评这项政策不公且成本高。
examples.teaching_note: assail a policy 常指言辞攻击。

examples.sentence: The army assailed the fortress at dawn.
examples.translation: 军队在黎明时猛攻堡垒。
examples.teaching_note: assail 也可指实际攻击。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 恐惧、疑虑、记忆等强烈地压过来。

examples.sentence: She was assailed by doubts before the final decision.
examples.translation: 在最终决定前，她被疑虑困扰。
examples.teaching_note: be assailed by doubts 是抽象用法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The article attacked the mayor’s record.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: attack 最普通；assail 更正式、力度更强。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Critics criticized the plan.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: criticize 更中性；assail 更猛烈。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The activists denounced the new law.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: denounce 强调公开强烈谴责。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The columnist lambasted the decision.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: lambaste 偏口语/新闻，表示痛批。

antonyms.example_sentence: The minister defended the policy.
The report praised the program.

confusable_words.example_sentence: The victim reported the assault.
confusable_words.difference_note: assault 更常作名词或法律上的袭击；assail 更书面。

word_family.example_sentence: Police arrested the assailant.
The assault shocked the city.

related_words.note: 语义迁移。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 适时的; 时机恰到好处的; 合时宜的 done or occurring at a favorable or useful time; well- timed: You've come at a most opportune moment. 你来得正是时候 。 | The time would seem to be opportune to negotiate a halt to the arms race. 看来这是通过谈判停止军备竞赛的大好时 HL. if 34} timely 3K inopportune /in'ppotju:n/ adj. 不凑巧的; AAM EA; 不合适的 not appropriate or convenient; happening at a bad time: an inopportune remark 4 4Et HW i& | The warplanes package could not have been proposed at a more inopportune time. 有关军用飞机的这一揽子交易可以说是在一个最不恰当的时刻提出来的

headline.core_en: happening at a suitable or advantageous time

familiar_bridge.summary: opportunity 是“机会”；opportune 形容某个时机刚好有利。

core.main: opportune 强调时间点合适，某件事出现得正是时候。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某个行动、到来或机会出现在有利时间点。

examples.sentence: Her opportune arrival prevented the argument from escalating.
examples.translation: 她及时到来，阻止了争论升级。
examples.teaching_note: opportune arrival 表示来得正是时候。

examples.sentence: The discovery came at an opportune moment for the researchers.
examples.translation: 这一发现对研究人员来说来得正是时候。
examples.teaching_note: at an opportune moment 是自然结构。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The timely warning saved lives.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: timely 更普通；opportune 强调时机有利。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The joke was well-timed.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: well-timed 更口语直观。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The talks began under propitious conditions.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: propitious 更正式，强调条件有利。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The ceremony marked an auspicious beginning.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: auspicious 更偏好兆头。

antonyms.example_sentence: His question came at an inopportune moment.
The announcement was untimely.

confusable_words.example_sentence: The company made an opportunistic purchase.
confusable_words.difference_note: opportunistic 指抓机会谋利，常带负面；opportune 指时机合适。

word_family.example_sentence: The trip offered an opportunity to study local culture.
The delay was inopportune.
The move seemed opportunistic.

related_words.note: 帮助理解 opportune 的核心。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 批评; 责备 the expression of disapproval or disappointment: She looked at him with reproach. 她责备地看着他 。 i X44] blame

headline.core_en: to express blame or disappointment; blame or disgrace

familiar_bridge.summary: approach 是接近；reproach 是带着责备去“面对”某人。

core.main: reproach 比 blame 更带失望或道德责备，也可作名词表示责备或耻辱。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 表达不满和失望，语气常比普通批评更个人化。

examples.sentence: She reproached him for ignoring the warning.
examples.translation: 她责备他无视警告。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

examples.sentence: His silence felt like a reproach.
examples.translation: 他的沉默像是一种责备。
examples.teaching_note: reproach 作名词可表示责备本身。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 名词可表示让人蒙羞的事物或名声上的污点。

examples.sentence: The scandal was a reproach to the institution.
examples.translation: 这桩丑闻是这个机构的耻辱。
examples.teaching_note: a reproach to... 表示让某方蒙羞。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: They blamed the error on poor planning.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: blame 更普通；reproach 带失望或道德责备。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report criticized the decision.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: criticize 可中性分析；reproach 更个人化。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The judge rebuked the lawyer sharply.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: rebuke 更正式、语气更重。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She chided him for being late.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: chide 比 reproach 轻一些。

antonyms.example_sentence: The teacher praised her effort.
The report commended the team’s honesty.

confusable_words.example_sentence: They approached the problem carefully.
confusable_words.difference_note: approach 与 reproach 词形相似但意义不同。

word_family.example_sentence: She gave him a reproachful look.

word_family.note: ir- 否定，表示不能被责备。
word_family.example_sentence: His conduct was irreproachable.

related_words.note: 正式近邻。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 无可指责的;无懈可击的 not deserving criticism or blame; having no fault: His private life was irreproachable. 他的私人生活无可指责

headline.core_cn: 无可指责的；无可挑剔的
headline.core_en: so good or proper that no criticism is possible

familiar_bridge.summary: reproach 是责备；irreproachable 就是“无法被责备”。

core.main: irreproachable 常形容 conduct、character、manners、record，表示行为或品格没有可批评之处。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调标准高到别人很难提出正当批评。

examples.sentence: Her conduct during the crisis was irreproachable.
examples.translation: 她在危机中的表现无可指责。
examples.teaching_note: 这个搭配很常见。

examples.sentence: The judge had an irreproachable record of public service.
examples.translation: 这位法官有无可挑剔的公共服务记录。
examples.teaching_note: record 可被 irreproachable 修饰。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He was blameless in the accident.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: blameless 更普通；irreproachable 更正式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The performance was nearly faultless.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: faultless 可用于表现或物品；irreproachable 偏行为品格。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her manners were impeccable.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: impeccable 接近，常形容品味、礼仪、表现。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His service was exemplary.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: exemplary 强调值得效仿。

antonyms.example_sentence: The decision was reproachable because it ignored the evidence.
The company was culpable for the accident.
antonyms.difference_note: culpable 更法律/道德责任感。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The fire caused irreparable damage.
confusable_words.difference_note: irreparable 是无法修复，不是无可指责。

word_family.example_sentence: She reproached him for the mistake.
He gave a reproachful glance.
She behaved irreproachably.

headline.core_en: using careful reasoning and examination of parts

familiar_bridge.summary: analysis 是“分析”；analytical 就是把整体拆开，按逻辑看结构和原因。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 提倡者; 主张者 an advocator or a promoter of (a belief or course of action): a leading proselytizer 一个主要的倡导者

headline.core_cn: 劝人改宗者；积极鼓吹者
headline.core_en: a person who tries to convert others to a belief, religion, or cause

familiar_bridge.summary: proselyte 是“皈依者/新信徒”；proselytizer 是主动劝别人加入某信仰或主张的人。

core.main: proselytizer 不只限宗教，也可指热心推销某种主义、运动或观念的人。

usage_groups.group_title: 劝别人接受信仰或主张
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调主动说服别人改变立场、加入某个阵营。

examples.sentence: The village resisted the efforts of outside proselytizers.
examples.translation: 村民抵制外来传教者的劝导。
examples.teaching_note: religious proselytizer 是原始常见义。

examples.sentence: He became a tireless proselytizer for renewable energy.
examples.translation: 他成了可再生能源不知疲倦的鼓吹者。
examples.teaching_note: 可引申到政治、技术或理念。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She is an advocate for prison reform.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: advocate 可中性；proselytizer 强调劝人转变信念。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The promoter advertised the new program.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: promoter 偏推广；proselytizer 更像思想/信仰劝化。

advanced_synonyms.meaning: 福音传播者；热情倡导者
advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He is a technology evangelist.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: evangelist 可宗教也可商业技术语境。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Missionaries established schools in the region.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: missionary 更明确宗教职业。

antonyms.example_sentence: Skeptics questioned the movement’s promises.
antonyms.difference_note: skeptic 不轻易接受或推广信念。

antonyms.example_sentence: Opponents rejected the proposal.

confusable_words.example_sentence: The proselyte joined the community.
confusable_words.difference_note: proselyte 是被转化的人；proselytizer 是劝别人转化的人。

word_family.example_sentence: The group tried to proselytize on campus.
The proselyte adopted the new faith.
The law restricted public proselytism.

related_words.note: 核心动作。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 【做某事所需的 ) 资金; 手段; 设施等 the money or other means needed for a particular purpose: have the wherewithal in cash to buy nuclear technology 有购买核技术的现款 | get the wherewithal for a project 4% 2! —li LE BAKA

headline.core_cn: 必要资金；必要手段或能力
headline.core_en: the money, means, or ability needed to do something

familiar_bridge.summary: wherewithal 不是普通 where，而是“做成某事所需要的东西”：钱、资源、能力都可以。

core.main: wherewithal 常和 have/lack the wherewithal 搭配，表示有没有条件完成某事。

usage_groups.group_title: 做事所需的资源或能力
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调完成目标需要的资金、工具、知识或能力是否具备。

examples.sentence: The town lacked the financial wherewithal to repair the bridge.
examples.translation: 这个小镇缺乏修桥所需的资金。
examples.teaching_note: financial wherewithal 是常见具体化表达。

examples.sentence: She had the wherewithal to turn a small idea into a successful company.
examples.translation: 她具备把一个小想法变成成功公司的能力和资源。
examples.teaching_note: wherewithal 可指能力加资源。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: They lacked the means to continue the project.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: means 最接近；wherewithal 更书面。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The school has limited resources.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: resources 偏具体资源；wherewithal 包括资源、钱和能力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The agency lacks the capacity to enforce the rule.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: capacity 强调能力或承载力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The team had the resources and ability to finish the work.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: 这个短语解释 wherewithal 的范围。

antonyms.example_sentence: His inability to pay ended the plan.
Poverty limited access to education.
antonyms.difference_note: 在财力语境中 poverty 可作对照。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Whereas costs rose, revenue fell.
confusable_words.difference_note: whereas 是连词，和 wherewithal 不同。

related_words.note: 最有用的熟词迁移。
related_words.example_sentence: They had the means to travel.

related_words.note: 帮助理解 wherewithal 的范围。
抽象能力义。

source.raw_ocr_text: #434)/7SL adj. 技艺高超的; 有才华的 highly trained or skilled: an accomplished pianist 技艺高超的钢琴家

headline.core_en: highly skilled or successful

familiar_bridge.summary: accomplish 是“完成”；accomplished 形容一个人完成了很多训练和成就，所以很有本事。

core.main: accomplished 常形容 artist、musician、writer、scholar，强调能力成熟、成就突出。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某人在某领域训练充分、表现出色。

examples.sentence: She is an accomplished pianist with an international reputation.
examples.translation: 她是一位享有国际声誉的杰出钢琴家。
examples.teaching_note: accomplished pianist 是自然搭配。

examples.sentence: The book was written by an accomplished historian.
examples.translation: 这本书由一位颇有成就的历史学家撰写。
examples.teaching_note: accomplished scholar/writer 等强调专业成就。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The team needs skilled workers.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: skilled 更普通；accomplished 强调高水平和成就。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She is a talented young actor.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: talented 强调天赋；accomplished 强调成熟成就。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He is proficient in three languages.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: proficient 强调达到熟练水平。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She is a consummate performer.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: consummate 更强，表示极其高超。

antonyms.example_sentence: The inexperienced driver made several mistakes.
antonyms.difference_note: inexperienced 与有成就/熟练相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The task is difficult for unskilled workers.
antonyms.difference_note: unskilled 直接表示技能不足。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Winning the award was a major accomplishment.
confusable_words.difference_note: accomplishment 是名词；accomplished 是形容词。

word_family.example_sentence: They accomplished the task on time.
Her accomplishments impressed the committee.
Several goals remained unaccomplished.

related_words.note: 帮助理解 accomplish 的基础动作。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 三眼的; 过于显眼的 noticeable or prominent in an unwelcome or intrusive way: We used less obtrusive colors in the bedjrooms. 卧室里用了不那么扎眼的颜色 。 #034] conspicuous

headline.core_cn: 突兀碍眼的；过分显眼的
headline.core_en: noticeable in an unpleasant or intrusive way

familiar_bridge.summary: intrude 是“闯入”；obtrusive 像某物闯进视线或空间，让人觉得碍事。

core.main: obtrusive 强调某物太显眼、太打扰，破坏环境或注意力。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调存在感过强，让人无法忽视，而且不舒服。

examples.sentence: The bright billboard was obtrusive in the quiet village.
examples.translation: 这块鲜亮广告牌在安静村庄里显得很突兀。
examples.teaching_note: obtrusive 强调不合环境、碍眼。

examples.sentence: The app was criticized for obtrusive advertising.
examples.translation: 这个应用因广告过于打扰而受到批评。
examples.teaching_note: obtrusive advertising 是自然搭配。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The stain was noticeable.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: noticeable 只是明显；obtrusive 是明显到打扰。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The questions felt intrusive.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: intrusive 更强调侵犯私人空间。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The red coat was conspicuous in the crowd.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: conspicuous 可中性；obtrusive 更负面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The procedure is less invasive than surgery.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: invasive 更强调侵入。

antonyms.example_sentence: The camera was small and unobtrusive.
The design uses subtle colors.

confusable_words.example_sentence: His response was obtuse.
confusable_words.difference_note: obtuse 是迟钝/钝角，不是突兀碍眼。

word_family.example_sentence: Personal opinions should not obtrude into the report.
The device is unobtrusive.
The logo was placed obtrusively on the page.

related_words.note: 熟词迁移。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.i. 变缓慢;， 变弛组;减弱 to become less active: The rain slackened. BH #5 J. | His business slackens in the winter. 在冬季他的生意清淡

headline.core_en: to become or make less tight, less strong, or slower

familiar_bridge.summary: slack 是“松的”；slacken 就是让紧张、速度或强度松下来。

core.main: slacken 可用于 rope/grip，也可用于 pace、effort、demand、growth 等抽象强度减弱。

usage_groups.group_title: 速度、力度或紧张度减弱
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调原来紧绷或强烈的状态变弱。

examples.sentence: The runner slackened his pace near the end of the race.
examples.translation: 赛跑接近尾声时，选手放慢了速度。
examples.teaching_note: slacken the pace 表示减速。

examples.sentence: Demand slackened after the holiday season.
examples.translation: 节假日过后需求减弱。
examples.teaching_note: demand slackened 是经济语境自然表达。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 物理上不再绷紧或抓得那么紧。

examples.sentence: She slackened her grip on the rope.
examples.translation: 她放松了抓绳子的手。
examples.teaching_note: 物理义帮助理解抽象义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The car slowed down near the turn.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: slow down 偏速度；slacken 可指速度、力度、紧张度。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He loosened the knot.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: loosen 偏物理变松。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The storm abated by morning.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: abate 更正式，常指风暴、疼痛、紧张减弱。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The effect diminished over time.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: diminish 更宽泛。

antonyms.example_sentence: The conflict intensified overnight.
The crew tightened the rope.

confusable_words.example_sentence: The rope was slack.
confusable_words.difference_note: slack 是形容词/名词；slacken 是动词。

word_family.example_sentence: The line went slack.
Slackness in enforcement caused problems.

related_words.note: 帮助理解物理义。

headline.core_en: a written record; to record or provide evidence

familiar_bridge.summary: document 作名词是文件；作动词是把事实记录下来，作为证据。

core.main: document 在学术和正式语境中常作动词，表示系统记录、证明某现象存在。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 指合同、报告、证件、档案等书面记录。

examples.sentence: The archive contains thousands of historical documents.
examples.translation: 档案馆保存着数千份历史文件。
examples.teaching_note: historical documents 是自然搭配。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 把事件、过程或现象记录下来，让它有证据可查。

examples.sentence: The study documents a sharp decline in insect populations.
examples.translation: 这项研究记录并证明昆虫数量急剧下降。
examples.teaching_note: document 作动词时常用于研究。

examples.sentence: Journalists documented the conditions inside the camp.
examples.translation: 记者记录了营地内部的状况。
examples.teaching_note: document 强调记录成证据。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The device records temperature every hour.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: record 更普通；document 更正式，常带证据价值。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The file contains the contract.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: file 是文件或归档；document 更宽。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The book chronicles the rise of the movement.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: chronicle 强调按时间叙述。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The data substantiate the claim.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: substantiate 强调提供证据支持。

antonyms.example_sentence: The files were erased.
The report concealed key facts.

confusable_words.example_sentence: The documentary documents the trial.
confusable_words.difference_note: documentary 是纪录片或纪实形容词。

word_family.example_sentence: The application requires documentation.
The documentary examines migration.
The effect is well documented.

related_words.note: document 作动词时常服务于证据。

headline.core_en: a film or program based on facts; factual rather than fictional

familiar_bridge.summary: document 是记录；documentary 就是基于真实记录和事实的作品。

core.main: documentary 最常作名词指纪录片，也可作形容词表示纪实性、文献性的。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 作品以真实人物、事件或资料为基础，而不是虚构故事。

examples.sentence: The documentary follows doctors working in remote villages.
examples.translation: 这部纪录片跟拍在偏远村庄工作的医生。

examples.sentence: She made a documentary about urban poverty.

examples.teaching_note: make a documentary 是自然结构。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调材料、证据或风格依赖真实记录。

examples.sentence: The historian relied on documentary evidence from the period.
examples.translation: 历史学家依靠那个时期的文献证据。
examples.teaching_note: documentary evidence 是正式表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The class watched a nonfiction film about climate change.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: nonfiction film 解释性强；documentary 更自然。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The footage provides a record of the event.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: record 是记录，不等于纪录片。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The book offers a factual account of the trial.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: factual account 强调基于事实。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The docudrama mixes fact and dramatization.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: docudrama 带戏剧化，不等同 documentary。

antonyms.example_sentence: The novel is fiction, not documentary.
The story was a fabrication.

confusable_words.example_sentence: The document was signed yesterday.
confusable_words.difference_note: document 是文件或记录动作；documentary 是纪录片/纪实的。

word_family.example_sentence: The report documents the change.
The claim lacks documentation.

related_words.note: 同语义场。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 427; 提供 supply or distribute (something): Licenses were issued indiscriminately to any company. 执照被随意颁发给公司 。 vt， 提出; 公开; 发表 ( 言论等 formally send out or make known: The minister issued a statement. 部长发表了一份声明 。 phr. take issue with 持异议; 不同意 disagree with; challenge: He took issue with me on my proposal. 他对我的建议持异议

headline.core_en: a topic or problem; to officially give or release

familiar_bridge.summary: issue 可以是“议题/问题”，也可以作动词表示官方发布。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 僵局 a situation in which no progress is possible, especially because of disagreement; a deadlock: The negotiation has reached an impasse. 谈判已陷入僵局

headline.core_en: a situation in which progress is impossible because people cannot agree

familiar_bridge.summary: pass 是通过；impasse 像没有路可通过，事情卡住了。

core.main: impasse 常出现在谈判、政治、争论中，表示双方无法继续前进。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 双方立场卡住，没有可接受的下一步。

core.main: skirt 是多义词：衣物、边缘、绕行、回避问题都很常见。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 路线不穿过中心，而是贴着边缘过去。

examples.sentence: The road skirts the edge of the lake.
examples.translation: 这条路沿着湖边绕行。
examples.teaching_note: skirt the edge 是物理义。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不正面面对，而是从旁边绕过去。

examples.sentence: The minister tried to skirt the issue during the interview.
examples.translation: 部长在采访中试图回避这个问题。
examples.teaching_note: skirt the issue 是核心抽象用法。

examples.sentence: The company was accused of skirting safety regulations.
examples.translation: 公司被指控规避安全规定。
examples.teaching_note: skirt regulations 表示规避规则。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 名词可指衣物，也可指某地区边缘。

examples.sentence: They built houses on the skirts of the forest.
examples.translation: 他们在森林边缘建了房子。
examples.teaching_note: skirts 可指边缘地带，较书面。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He avoided the question.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: avoid 最普通；skirt 更有绕开、不正面处理的画面。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: We went around the flooded road.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: go around 是物理绕行。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The company tried to circumvent the rule.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: circumvent 更正式，常指规避规则。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The witness evaded the question.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: evade 更强调有意躲避。

antonyms.example_sentence: She confronted the problem directly.
The report addresses the issue.
antonyms.difference_note: address 与 skirt the issue 相对。

confusable_words.example_sentence: He wore a blue shirt.
confusable_words.difference_note: 拼写相近但无关。

word_family.note: 和 skirt 的边缘义相关。
word_family.example_sentence: They live on the outskirts of the city.
The room had wooden skirting.

related_words.note: 高价值近邻。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 支柱; 主要依靠 a thing on which something else is based or depends: Cotton is the mainstay of the surrounding country. 棉花是周围农村的主要收益

headline.core_en: the main support or most important part of something

familiar_bridge.summary: main 是主要，stay 有支撑感；mainstay 就是最主要的支撑。

core.main: mainstay 常指某个行业、组织、饮食或经济结构中最可靠、最核心的支柱。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某人或某物长期支撑一个系统、组织或生活方式。

examples.sentence: Tourism is a mainstay of the island’s economy.
examples.translation: 旅游业是这个岛屿经济的支柱。
examples.teaching_note: 这个搭配很常见。

examples.sentence: Rice remains the mainstay of the local diet.
examples.translation: 大米仍是当地饮食的主要支柱。
examples.teaching_note: mainstay of the diet 表示主要依靠的食物。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Public support kept the project alive.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: support 更普通；mainstay 是最主要、最可靠的支撑。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Education is a pillar of democracy.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: pillar 接近，但更常作比喻支柱。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Small farms are the backbone of the region.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: backbone 强调核心支撑力量。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Trust is the cornerstone of the agreement.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: cornerstone 强调基础性。

antonyms.example_sentence: The fee is only a minor part of the cost.
antonyms.difference_note: minor part 与 mainstay 的核心地位相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The old system became a liability.

confusable_words.example_sentence: The idea entered the mainstream.
confusable_words.difference_note: mainstream 是主流，不是支柱。

word_family.example_sentence: The main problem is cost.
The rope stays the mast.

related_words.note: 高价值近邻。
related_words.example_sentence: The university is a pillar of the town.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 使热情; 使有活力 give vitality and enthusiasm to: People were energized by his ideas. 他的

headline.core_en: to give someone or something energy, enthusiasm, or renewed activity

familiar_bridge.summary: energy 是“能量”；energize 就是把能量注入人、群体或行动里。

core.main: energize 强调让原本低沉、停滞或缺少动力的人或事情重新活跃起来。

usage_groups.group_title: 让人或群体重新有动力
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于演讲、想法、领导者或事件激发人的热情和行动力。

examples.sentence: The candidate's speech energized young voters who had felt ignored.
examples.translation: 候选人的演讲激励了那些原本觉得被忽视的年轻选民。
examples.teaching_note: energize voters 强调让一群人重新愿意行动。

examples.sentence: A clear goal can energize a tired team near the end of a project.
examples.translation: 一个清晰的目标能在项目后期让疲惫的团队重新有干劲。
examples.teaching_note: 这里不是给身体供能，而是激发精神和行动力。

usage_groups.group_title: 让系统或活动重新活跃
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于政策、投资、改革等给机构、市场或计划注入新的活力。

examples.sentence: The new investment helped energize the local economy.
examples.translation: 新的投资帮助当地经济重新活跃起来。
examples.teaching_note: energize the economy 是正式语境中的自然搭配。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The coach motivated the players before the final game.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: motivate 更偏激发做事动机；energize 更强调让人精神和行动都活跃起来。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her words encouraged him to keep working.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: encourage 是给信心；energize 更有注入活力的感觉。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The crisis galvanized citizens into demanding reform.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: galvanize 更强，常指突然激发集体行动。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The new director invigorated the museum's public programs.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: invigorate 更书面，常用于机构、计划或精神状态。

antonyms.example_sentence: The long meeting drained everyone's energy.
antonyms.difference_note: drain 是把能量耗掉，与 energize 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The programme aims to energise local communities.
confusable_words.difference_note: energise 是英式拼写，意思与 energize 相同。

word_family.example_sentence: The project requires time and energy.

word_family.note: 形容人或活动很有活力。
word_family.example_sentence: She is an energetic teacher.

word_family.note: 常形容活动、经历或想法让人恢复活力。
word_family.example_sentence: The workshop was surprisingly energizing.

related_words.note: 同样表示给人或组织注入活力。
related_words.example_sentence: Fresh leadership can invigorate an old institution.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 学究; 书呆子; 过分关注细节或喜欢卖弄学问的人 a person who is excessively concerned with minor details and rules or with displaying academic learning: What we need is someone with common sense, not a pedant. 我们所需要的是一个有常识的人 ， 而不是书呆子. 派生 pedantic /pr'deentik/ adj. 学究气的 of or like a pedant: a pedantic attention to details 学究式地注意细枝未节

headline.core_cn: 学究；卖弄学问、拘泥细节的人
headline.core_en: a person too concerned with minor rules, details, or displaying learning

familiar_bridge.summary: pedant 不是普通的 scholar，而是那种过分纠缠规则和细节、让人觉得烦的“学究”。

core.main: pedant 带贬义，批评一个人把知识、规则或细节看得过重，却缺少常识或实际判断。

usage_groups.group_title: 拘泥细节或规则的学究
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于批评一个人过分纠结小错误、定义或规则，而忽略实际问题。

examples.sentence: What we need is someone with common sense, not a pedant who argues over every comma.
examples.translation: 我们需要的是有常识的人，而不是一个为每个逗号争论的学究。
examples.teaching_note: pedant 常和 common sense 形成对比。

examples.sentence: The professor was brilliant, but his students feared he would become a pedant over minor mistakes.
examples.translation: 这位教授很聪明，但学生担心他会在小错误上显得过分学究气。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调“过分纠细节”的负面语气。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: No one wanted to work with a know-it-all who corrected every remark.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: know-it-all 更口语，强调自以为懂；pedant 更强调卖弄知识和拘泥细节。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She is a stickler for accuracy in citations.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: stickler 可以中性或褒义；pedant 通常更贬义。

advanced_synonyms.meaning: 教条主义的；死守理论的人
advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The committee rejected his doctrinaire approach to policy.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: doctrinaire 强调死守理论或意识形态；pedant 强调细节和学问上的卖弄。

antonyms.example_sentence: A pragmatist would solve the problem instead of debating the definition.
antonyms.difference_note: pragmatist 重视实际效果，和 pedant 的空泛纠细节相对。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The scholar spent years studying medieval law.
confusable_words.difference_note: scholar 是中性或褒义；pedant 是批评式说法。

word_family.note: 形容语言、态度或人过分强调规则和细节。
word_family.example_sentence: The review criticized the book's pedantic tone.

word_family.note: 指这种令人厌烦的态度或行为。
word_family.example_sentence: The lecture collapsed into pedantry.

related_words.note: 同源于与教育相关的词根，但现代意思不同。

source.raw_ocr_text: 37g 4X v.t. (也作 fence in) 束缚; 限制 to restrict the activity of: She felt fenced in by domestic routine. 她觉得被家务束缚住了

headline.core_cn: 围住；限制；回避正面回答
headline.core_en: to enclose or restrict; also to avoid giving a direct answer

familiar_bridge.summary: fence 本来是“篱笆”；动词里常有“围起来、挡住、限制住”的感觉。

core.main: 本词条重点是 fence in：像被篱笆圈住一样，活动、选择或生活空间受到限制。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调人或行动空间被制度、日常事务、家庭责任等限制。

examples.sentence: She felt fenced in by domestic routine and longed for work outside the home.
examples.translation: 她觉得被家务日常束缚住了，渴望到家外工作。
examples.teaching_note: feel fenced in 是本义项最自然的表达。

examples.sentence: Strict rules fenced the researchers in and left little room for creative experiments.
examples.translation: 严格规定限制了研究人员，让他们几乎没有空间做创造性实验。
examples.teaching_note: 这里把规则比作围栏，限制行动空间。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 在 fence with / fence about 中，表示说话绕开重点，不直接承诺。

examples.sentence: The minister fenced with reporters' questions instead of giving a clear answer.
examples.translation: 部长没有明确回答，而是绕开记者的问题。
examples.teaching_note: 这个义项较正式，重点是回避正面回应。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The law restricts access to private records.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: restrict 是普通“限制”；fence in 更有被围住、受困的画面。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The illness confined him to his room for a week.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: confine 更正式，强调被限定在空间或范围内。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The treaty circumscribed the country's military options.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: circumscribe 更书面，常用于权力、范围、选择被限制。

antonyms.example_sentence: The reform freed local schools from unnecessary rules.
antonyms.difference_note: free 与 fence in 的“束缚住”相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The children learned to fend for themselves.
confusable_words.difference_note: fend 与 fence 形近，但意思不同。

word_family.note: 形容人或空间被限制住。
word_family.example_sentence: He hated the fenced-in feeling of the small office.

word_family.note: 与 fence 的名词义有关。
word_family.example_sentence: The garden was surrounded by wooden fencing.

related_words.note: 和“圈住、限制空间”的画面相近。
related_words.example_sentence: The animals were kept in a large enclosure.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 细致的; —244\%j8) careful about doing what is honest and morally right: work with scrupulous care 一丝不苟地工作 adj. 道德上严于律已的; 极为正直的 very concerned to avoid doing wrong: Less scrupulous companies find ways to evade the law. 不正直的公司想办法钻法律漏洞

headline.core_cn: 一丝不苟的；严守道德原则的
headline.core_en: very careful about details or about doing what is morally right

familiar_bridge.summary: scruple 是“道德顾虑”；scrupulous 可以从“非常有顾虑、非常小心”理解。

core.main: scrupulous 有两个常见方向：做事极细致，或在道德上非常谨慎正直。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调做事时标准很高，检查细节认真到几乎不放过任何疏漏。

examples.sentence: The historian checked every quotation with scrupulous care.
examples.translation: 这位历史学家一丝不苟地核对了每一处引文。
examples.teaching_note: scrupulous care 强调细节上的严谨。

examples.sentence: Her scrupulous attention to detail made the report unusually reliable.
examples.translation: 她对细节的一丝不苟让这份报告格外可靠。
examples.teaching_note: 这里是褒义，表示认真细致。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 形容人、公司或行为努力避免欺骗、不公或违法。

examples.sentence: Less scrupulous companies find ways to evade the law.
examples.translation: 不那么正直的公司会想办法钻法律空子。
examples.teaching_note: less scrupulous 常用于批评不守道德或规则的人。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She made a careful comparison of the two studies.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: careful 很普通；scrupulous 更强调极高标准和细节意识。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: An honest official refused the bribe.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: honest 直接说诚实；scrupulous 强调主动避免不正当行为。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The editor made meticulous corrections to the manuscript.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: meticulous 主要是细节精确；scrupulous 还可指道德谨慎。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: A conscientious researcher reports negative results as well as positive ones.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: conscientious 强调责任感；scrupulous 强调细节或道德边界。

antonyms.example_sentence: Careless record-keeping weakened the study.

antonyms.meaning: 不择手段的；无道德顾忌的
antonyms.example_sentence: Unscrupulous dealers sold fake medicine to desperate patients.
antonyms.difference_note: unscrupulous 是 scrupulous 的直接反义，常指无道德底线。

confusable_words.example_sentence: She had no scruple about using private letters as evidence.
confusable_words.difference_note: scruple 是名词；scrupulous 是形容词。

word_family.example_sentence: He betrayed them without a scruple.

word_family.meaning: 无道德顾忌的；不择手段的
word_family.note: un- 加在前面，表示缺少道德顾虑。
word_family.example_sentence: Unscrupulous lenders exploited poor families.

word_family.meaning: 一丝不苟地；严守原则地
word_family.example_sentence: The accounts were scrupulously maintained.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 11; 托词 a false reason that you give for doing something, usually something bad, in order to hide the real reason; an excuse: find a pretext for 为 … 找借口

headline.core_en: a false or misleading reason given to hide the real reason for an action

familiar_bridge.summary: text 是“说出来的内容”；pretext 像摆在前面的说法，用来遮住真正原因。

core.main: pretext 不是普通 reason，而是带掩饰意味的借口：表面理由和真实动机不一致。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于政治、争论、行动或惩罚中，表面理由只是遮掩真实动机。

examples.sentence: The government closed the newspaper under the pretext of protecting public order.
examples.translation: 政府以维护公共秩序为借口关闭了那家报纸。
examples.teaching_note: 这个搭配暗示理由不真诚。

examples.sentence: He found a pretext for leaving the meeting early.
examples.translation: 他找了个借口提前离开会议。
examples.teaching_note: find a pretext for 表示“为某事找托词”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She made an excuse for missing the appointment.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: excuse 可真可假；pretext 更明确暗示用假理由遮掩。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report gave several reasons for the delay.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: reason 中性；pretext 带欺骗或掩饰意味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The invitation was a ruse to get him out of the building.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: ruse 更强调欺骗性的计谋；pretext 强调表面理由。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: They entered the house under the pretense of checking the meter.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: pretense 和 pretext 接近，但 pretense 还常指假装的状态。

antonyms.example_sentence: The real reason for the policy was never announced.
antonyms.difference_note: real reason 与 pretext 的表面借口相对。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The sentence is hard to understand without context.
confusable_words.difference_note: context 是背景信息；pretext 是掩饰真实动机的理由。

word_family.note: 和 pretext 语义接近，都有表面说法与真实情况不一致的感觉。
word_family.example_sentence: He smiled under the pretense of politeness.

word_family.note: 帮助理解“表面装出来”的语义场。
word_family.example_sentence: The child pretended to be asleep.

related_words.note: 构词联想：pretext 是摆在前面的说法，不一定是真正理由。
related_words.example_sentence: The text of the speech was released later.

source.raw_ocr_text: 2444/48 SL adj. 新的; #r#if; 新奇的 new or unusual in an interesting way: a novel idea 新想法 #5044 original, innovative,experimental, unprecedented wR novelty /npvalty n. 新颖; 新奇 the quality of being new, original, or unusual: Afier the novelty of washing dishes wore off, she did not want to do it anymore. 洗盘子工作的新奇感消失之后 ， 她就不再想干了 。 5044] originality RE novelty /npvlty n. 新事物;新奇的东西 a new or unfamiliar thing or experience: In 1914 air travel was still a novelty. 在 1914 年坐飞机是件新鲜事

headline.core_en: new and unusual in an interesting way; also a long work of fiction

familiar_bridge.summary: new 只是“新的”；novel 更强调“新颖、有意思、以前不常见”。

core.main: GRE 中 novel 常作形容词，表示观点、方法或设计新颖，不是普通的 new；它也可以作名词“小说”。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调不只是新出现，而是有新意、能带来不同理解或方法。

examples.sentence: The researchers proposed a novel approach to storing solar energy.
examples.translation: 研究人员提出了一种储存太阳能的新颖方法。
examples.teaching_note: novel approach 是学术语境中的常见搭配。

examples.sentence: Her novel interpretation challenged the standard view of the poem.
examples.translation: 她的新颖解读挑战了对这首诗的传统看法。
examples.teaching_note: novel 强调观点有新意，不只是最近出现。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 作名词时，novel 指长篇虚构叙事作品。

examples.sentence: The novel follows three families during a period of political change.
examples.translation: 这部小说讲述了政治变动时期三个家庭的故事。
examples.teaching_note: 名词义很常见，但本单词书重点通常是形容词义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The team tested a new method for measuring sleep.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: new 只说明最近出现；novel 强调新颖、有独创性。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The experiment produced an unusual result.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unusual 只是不常见；novel 通常带“有意思的新意”。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The company developed an innovative way to recycle plastic.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: innovative 更强调创造性改进；novel 更强调新颖和不寻常。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The court faced an unprecedented legal question.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: unprecedented 比 novel 更强，强调没有先例。

antonyms.example_sentence: The study used a conventional method of analysis.
antonyms.difference_note: conventional 与 novel 的新颖相对。

antonyms.example_sentence: The argument followed a familiar pattern.
antonyms.difference_note: familiar 强调已经见过，不再新奇。

confusable_words.example_sentence: A novice may need clear instructions.
confusable_words.difference_note: novice 是人，指新手；novel 是新颖的或小说。

word_family.note: novel 的相关名词，指新鲜感或新奇事物。
word_family.example_sentence: The novelty of the job soon wore off.

word_family.note: 来自 novel 的名词义。
word_family.example_sentence: The novelist set the story in a small coastal town.

related_words.note: 同在“新方法、新想法”的语义场。
related_words.example_sentence: Small companies often innovate faster than large ones.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. AIK; 顾虑; 良心的责备 a feeling of guilt or moral scruple that prevents or follows the doing of something bad: The student seemed to have no compunctions about cheating. 这个学 4 Xt TE BEL — Bt, RB) AK. | compunctions of conscience Rit hit %

headline.core_cn: 内疚；良心不安；道德顾虑
headline.core_en: a feeling of guilt or moral unease about doing something wrong

familiar_bridge.summary: compunction 可以理解成良心突然“刺”一下：做坏事前后感到不安。

core.main: compunction 强调道德上的不安或内疚，常用于 have no compunction about doing sth，表示毫无顾忌。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 描述人在欺骗、伤害、背叛或不公行为前后感到的道德顾虑。
common_patterns: have no compunction about doing something

examples.sentence: The student seemed to have no compunction about cheating on the exam.
examples.translation: 这个学生似乎对考试作弊毫无良心不安。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

examples.sentence: She felt a sudden compunction after lying to her closest friend.
examples.translation: 她对最亲近的朋友撒谎后突然感到良心不安。
examples.teaching_note: compunction 常和 guilt、moral unease 相关。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He felt guilt after ignoring the warning.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: guilt 更普通；compunction 更强调良心上的迟疑或不安。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The thief showed no remorse for his actions.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: remorse 通常更强，偏事后的深切悔恨。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She had no qualms about criticizing the plan in public.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: qualm 可指道德或实际上的疑虑；compunction 更偏道德内疚。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He had few scruples about using false evidence.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: scruple 常指阻止人做坏事的道德顾虑。

antonyms.example_sentence: His shamelessness shocked even his allies.
antonyms.difference_note: shamelessness 是完全缺少羞耻或良心不安。

word_family.meaning: 感到内疚的；良心不安的
word_family.note: 较少见，但和 compunction 直接同族。
word_family.example_sentence: He sounded compunctious after the damage was done.

related_words.note: compunction 常来自 conscience 的不安。
related_words.example_sentence: Her conscience would not let her stay silent.

related_words.note: 和 compunction 都表示良心上的迟疑。
related_words.example_sentence: He had no scruple about breaking the promise.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 轻信的; 容易受骗的 easily persuaded to believe something; credulous: gullible persons & 易受骗的人

headline.core_en: too ready to believe what others say and therefore easy to deceive

familiar_bridge.summary: gullible 像“别人一说就信”，重点不是善良，而是判断太容易被带走。

core.main: gullible 用来形容人缺少怀疑精神，容易相信谎言、广告、骗局或荒唐说法。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 形容 person、audience、consumer 等很容易被说服或欺骗。

examples.sentence: The scam targeted gullible investors who trusted promises of quick profit.
examples.translation: 这个骗局瞄准了那些轻信快速获利承诺的投资者。
examples.teaching_note: gullible investors 是自然搭配，强调易受骗。

examples.sentence: A gullible reader may accept the rumor without checking the facts.
examples.translation: 轻信的读者可能不核实事实就接受这个谣言。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调缺少怀疑和核查。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He was trusting enough to lend money to a stranger.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: trusting 可带善意；gullible 更批评判断力不足。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: It was naive to believe every promise in the advertisement.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: naive 强调经验不足；gullible 强调容易被骗。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The article warns against a credulous acceptance of miracle cures.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: credulous 更书面，常指不加怀疑地相信。

antonyms.example_sentence: A skeptical audience demanded evidence for every claim.
antonyms.difference_note: skeptical 会要求证据，与 gullible 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Consumers should be wary of offers that sound too good to be true.
antonyms.difference_note: wary 强调警惕，不轻易相信。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The guilty official resigned after the investigation.
confusable_words.difference_note: guilty 形近但意思完全不同。

word_family.example_sentence: The fraud depended on public gullibility.

related_words.note: 正式近义词，也可和 credible/credence 一起记。
related_words.example_sentence: A credulous public may spread false stories quickly.

related_words.note: in- 表否定，和 gullible 的态度相反。
related_words.example_sentence: She gave him an incredulous look.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. (做法 、 信念等背后的 ) 理由; 依据 a set of reasons or a logical basis for a course of action or a particular belief: It is the rationale for comprehensive schools. 这就是设立综合中学 a) 22 44 (KAR. | [will put before the public what our foreign policy is and explain its rationale. 我愿将我国外交政策的内容公诸于众 ， 并说明其依据

headline.core_cn: 理由；根本依据；逻辑说明
headline.core_en: the set of reasons or logical basis behind a decision, policy, belief, or action

familiar_bridge.summary: rational 是“理性的”；rationale 是某个决定背后的“理性理由或逻辑”。

core.main: rationale 不只是一个简单 reason，而是支撑做法、政策或信念的一整套理由。

usage_groups.group_title: 解释政策、决定或做法背后的逻辑
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调某个决定背后有一套可以说明其合理性的逻辑。

examples.sentence: The report explains the rationale for closing several small schools.
examples.translation: 报告解释了关闭几所小学校的理由。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

examples.sentence: Before adopting the policy, the committee asked for a clearer rationale.
examples.translation: 在采纳这项政策之前，委员会要求给出更清楚的依据。
examples.teaching_note: rationale 常用于正式讨论政策或决定。

usage_groups.group_title: 说明某种信念或理论的依据
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于学术或论证语境，说明一个观点背后的逻辑基础。

examples.sentence: The author gives a moral rationale for protecting endangered species.
examples.translation: 作者为保护濒危物种给出了道德层面的理由。
examples.teaching_note: 这里的 rationale 是一套支持观点的理由。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She gave a practical reason for delaying the trip.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: reason 可以很简单；rationale 更像系统性的理由或逻辑基础。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The decision had no clear legal basis.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: basis 强调依据；rationale 强调解释这一依据的逻辑。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The government offered no convincing justification for the ban.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: justification 常带为某事辩护的意味；rationale 更中性地说明背后逻辑。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The argument rests on a doubtful premise.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: premise 是论证起点；rationale 是一套理由。

antonyms.example_sentence: The security concern was merely a pretext for censorship.
antonyms.difference_note: pretext 是表面借口；rationale 应是真正的理由或逻辑。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The debate questioned the rationality of the decision.
confusable_words.difference_note: rationality 是理性程度；rationale 是具体理由。

word_family.note: rationale 的基础语义来自 reason 和 logic。
word_family.example_sentence: A rational decision should consider evidence.

word_family.note: 表示一个判断或行为是否符合理性。
word_family.example_sentence: The study questioned the rationality of the policy.

word_family.note: 常指事后给行为找看似合理的解释。
word_family.example_sentence: He tried to rationalize his failure by blaming the weather.

related_words.note: rationale 常体现一段 reasoning。
related_words.example_sentence: Her reasoning was careful but incomplete.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 使 … 合理; O4-- HE attempt to explain or justify (one's own or another's behavior or attitude) with logical, plausible reasons, even if these are not true or appropriate: He rationalized his cheating by saying that everyone else cheated. {t1,¥| K RA {EBA A BERET 3 444 justify, explain

headline.core_en: to explain or justify behavior with reasons that may sound logical but may hide the real motive

familiar_bridge.summary: rational 是“理性的”；rationalize 常是事后把不理性的行为包装成“看起来有理”。

core.main: rationalize 不等于普通 explain。它常暗示一个人先做了某事，再找一套听起来合理的理由来替自己辩解。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于解释错误、懒惰、作弊、偏见等行为时，常带自我辩解意味。

examples.sentence: He rationalized his cheating by saying that everyone else cheated.
examples.translation: 他为自己的作弊找理由，说别人也都作弊。
examples.teaching_note: rationalize by saying... 是典型结构，暗示理由站不住脚。

examples.sentence: She tried to rationalize the purchase as an investment, though she simply wanted it.
examples.translation: 她试图把这次购买说成投资，其实只是自己想买。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调把真实动机包装成合理理由。

usage_groups.group_title: 把制度或流程合理化、精简化
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 在商业或管理语境中，rationalize 也可表示让组织、流程更有效率。

examples.sentence: The company tried to rationalize production by closing inefficient factories.
examples.translation: 公司试图通过关闭低效工厂来精简生产体系。
examples.teaching_note: 这个义项更中性，表示让系统更理顺。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He tried to justify the delay by citing safety concerns.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: justify 可合理也可不合理；rationalize 常暗示理由是事后包装。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The official tried to explain away the missing documents.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: explain away 和 rationalize 都可带回避责任的意味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The regime used old laws to legitimize its new restrictions.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: legitimize 强调赋予正当性；rationalize 强调给行为找理由。

antonyms.example_sentence: She admitted that the decision had been selfish.
antonyms.difference_note: admit 是承认真相；rationalize 是把行为包装成合理。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The committee asked for the rationale behind the plan.
confusable_words.difference_note: rationale 是名词，指理由本身；rationalize 是动词，指给行为找理由。

word_family.note: rationalize 表面上是在制造 rational 的解释。
word_family.example_sentence: A rational argument should face the evidence.

word_family.note: rationalize 常会制造一个 rationale。
word_family.example_sentence: The report gave no clear rationale for the change.

word_family.note: 指这种找理由的过程或结果。
word_family.example_sentence: His explanation sounded like a rationalization after the fact.

related_words.note: rationalize 和 reason 有关联，但它常带自我辩解色彩。
related_words.example_sentence: A good reason should survive criticism.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. WEE; 解码 convert (a coded message) into intelligible language: J was involved in decoding enemy documents. 4% C4 BOE EE KF.

headline.core_cn: 解码；破译；理解隐含信息
headline.core_en: to convert coded information into understandable form; to interpret something hidden or difficult

familiar_bridge.summary: code 是“编码”；decode 就是把隐藏或加密的信息还原成看得懂的内容。

core.main: decode 可以是真正破译密码，也可以表示理解表情、文本、行为背后的含义。

usage_groups.group_title: 把密码或编码信息变成可读内容
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于文件、信号、数据、敌方情报等从编码状态转成可理解语言。

examples.sentence: Analysts worked through the night to decode the enemy documents.
examples.translation: 分析人员通宵工作，破译敌方文件。
examples.teaching_note: decode enemy documents 强调把加密或难懂的文件还原成可理解的信息。

examples.sentence: The software can decode encrypted files only with the correct key.
examples.translation: 这个软件只有在有正确密钥时才能解码加密文件。
examples.teaching_note: 这里是技术语境中的解码。

usage_groups.group_title: 理解隐藏含义或复杂信息
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于表情、行为、文本或社会信号，需要读出表面之下的信息。

examples.sentence: Children gradually learn to decode facial expressions and tone of voice.
examples.translation: 孩子会逐渐学会理解面部表情和语气中的含义。
examples.teaching_note: decode 在这里是“解读隐含信息”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The critic interpreted the painting as a response to war.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: interpret 更广泛；decode 强调从隐藏、编码或不易懂的信息中读出意思。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The program translates speech into text.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: translate 是从一种语言或形式转成另一种；decode 更强调破解编码。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Scholars finally deciphered the ancient inscription.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: decipher 和 decode 很接近，但常用于难读文字、密码或古文字。

antonyms.example_sentence: The device encodes the signal before sending it.
antonyms.difference_note: encode 是把信息转成编码；decode 是反向解码。

word_family.example_sentence: The message was written in code.

word_family.note: en- 表示使进入某状态；encode 是把信息变成代码。
word_family.example_sentence: The system encodes each password before storage.

word_family.note: 执行 decode 的人或工具。
word_family.example_sentence: The decoder translated the signal into text.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.i. 不发展; 停滞 to become or remain stagnant: The economy stagnated as a result of these tax measures. 这些税收政策造成了经济发展停顿 。 | The negotiations stagnated. 谈判陷入便局 。 WR stagnation /stegmerfan/ n. 不发展; 停滞 lack of activity, growth, or development: economic stagnation 4 If KEANE H

headline.core_en: to stop developing, growing, or making progress

familiar_bridge.summary: stagnant water 是“不流动的死水”；stagnate 就像发展也变成死水，不再前进。

core.main: stagnate 常用于经济、谈判、事业、思想等失去增长或进展。

usage_groups.group_title: 经济、谈判或事业停滞不前
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调原本应该发展或推进的事情停住了，缺少活力和变化。

examples.sentence: The economy stagnated after several years of weak investment.
examples.translation: 在多年投资疲弱之后，经济陷入停滞。
examples.teaching_note: the economy stagnated 是常见正式表达。

examples.sentence: The negotiations stagnated when neither side would compromise.
examples.translation: 双方都不愿妥协时，谈判陷入停滞。
examples.teaching_note: negotiations stagnate 表示进程停住。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The rain stopped before noon.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: stop 很普通；stagnate 强调发展、增长或活动长期停滞。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The talks stalled over a dispute about borders.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: stall 常指进程卡住；stagnate 更偏长期没有发展。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The project languished for years without funding.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: languish 更有无力衰败感。

antonyms.example_sentence: The town developed rapidly after the railway opened.
antonyms.difference_note: develop 与 stagnate 相反，表示持续增长或进步。

antonyms.example_sentence: Local businesses flourished as tourism increased.

word_family.note: 形容水、空气或发展状态不动。
word_family.example_sentence: The stagnant water attracted mosquitoes.
Economic stagnation lasted for a decade.

related_words.note: 学术语境中也表示静止、停滞。
related_words.example_sentence: The society seemed trapped in political stasis.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 彻底消灭; 根除 killing, especially of a whole group of people or animals: extermination of household pests 根除房子里的害虫

headline.core_en: the complete killing or destruction of a group of people, animals, pests, or threats

familiar_bridge.summary: terminate 是“结束”；extermination 是把某一类生命或威胁彻底消灭。

core.main: extermination 语气很重，可用于害虫根除，也可用于历史和暴力语境中的大规模杀戮。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调清除对象不是减少一点，而是被彻底消灭或根除。

examples.sentence: The city began a campaign for the extermination of disease-carrying mosquitoes.
examples.translation: 该市发起了一场消灭传播疾病蚊子的行动。
examples.teaching_note: 这里是公共卫生语境。

examples.sentence: The landlord hired a company for the extermination of household pests.
examples.translation: 房东雇了一家公司来根除屋内害虫。
examples.teaching_note: extermination of household pests 来自原书语境。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于战争、种族暴力或极端政策，语气强烈而严肃。

examples.sentence: The historian described the policy as an attempt at systematic extermination.
examples.translation: 历史学家把这项政策描述为系统性灭绝的企图。
examples.teaching_note: 这个义项非常严肃，不可轻描淡写使用。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The new rule led to the elimination of several fees.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: elimination 可指普通消除；extermination 多指彻底杀灭生命群体。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The storm caused the destruction of several bridges.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: destruction 范围更广；extermination 特指彻底杀灭或根除。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The campaign aimed at the eradication of smallpox.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: eradication 常用于疾病、贫困、问题等；extermination 更强调杀灭。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The army faced annihilation after the failed retreat.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: annihilation 更宏大强烈，可用于军队、文明或物质毁灭。

antonyms.example_sentence: The reserve was created for the protection of endangered species.
antonyms.difference_note: protection 与 extermination 的破坏性方向相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The chemicals were used to exterminate insects.

word_family.note: 执行 pest extermination 的人。
word_family.example_sentence: The exterminator inspected the basement.

related_words.note: 和 exterminate 共享“结束”的核心，但 exterminate 是彻底消灭生命或群体。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. Ha(t; 代替 supersede and replace: supplant the teachings of rule of love with the practice of the rule of fear 以实行恐怖治来取代宣扬爱的统治 | Machinery has largely supplanted hand labor in making shoes. 在制鞋业中 ， 机器已基本替代了手工劳动

headline.core_cn: 取代；替代并挤掉原有事物
headline.core_en: to replace something, especially by taking over the position it once held

familiar_bridge.summary: plant 有“放置、扎根”的感觉；supplant 像后来者把原来占位的东西挤下去。

core.main: supplant 比 replace 更正式，常指新技术、新制度、新观念逐渐取代旧的。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于技术、制度、观念或方法替代旧形式。

examples.sentence: Machinery has largely supplanted hand labor in making shoes.
examples.translation: 在制鞋业中，机器已基本取代了手工劳动。
examples.teaching_note: supplant hand labor 是原书中的自然用法。

examples.sentence: Digital archives may eventually supplant many paper records.
examples.translation: 数字档案最终可能取代许多纸质记录。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调新形式占据旧形式的位置。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: New software replaced the old accounting system.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: replace 是普通替换；supplant 更正式，强调后来者占据原位置。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Online sales took over much of the retail market.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: take over 更口语，强调接管控制。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The new regulation superseded the earlier rule.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: supersede 和 supplant 接近，但更常用于规则、文件、标准使旧者失效。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: New technology displaced many routine jobs.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: displace 强调把原有者挤走；supplant 更强调新旧更替。

antonyms.example_sentence: The museum preserved many traditional crafts.

word_family.note: supplant 的动名词，可描述新旧替换过程。
word_family.example_sentence: The supplanting of hand labor took decades.

related_words.note: 构词联想：supplant 可理解为后来者“种到原位置上”。
related_words.example_sentence: They planted trees along the road.

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. ck; 极大数量 a countless or extremely great number: a myriad of stars We KEE

headline.core_en: a countless or extremely large number; countless or very many

familiar_bridge.summary: myriad 比 many 更书面、更夸张，像“数不清的一大堆”。

core.main: myriad 可作名词 a myriad of，也可作形容词 myriad problems / forms。

usage_groups.group_title: 数量极多、难以一一列举
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调数量大到难以逐一数清，常带书面或文学色彩。

examples.sentence: A myriad of stars appeared as the sky grew darker.
examples.translation: 天色渐暗时，无数星星显现出来。
examples.teaching_note: a myriad of 是经典结构。

examples.sentence: The proposal created myriad practical problems for local officials.
examples.translation: 这项提案给地方官员带来了无数实际问题。
examples.teaching_note: myriad 作形容词时直接修饰复数名词。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Many students found the assignment difficult.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: many 最普通；myriad 更书面，数量感更强。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Countless small errors weakened the argument.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: countless 和 myriad 接近，都强调多到难数。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The archive contains innumerable letters from the period.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: innumerable 更正式，强调无法计数。

antonyms.example_sentence: Few people attended the meeting.
antonyms.difference_note: few 与 myriad 的“大量”相反。

word_family.note: 较少见，了解即可，不是重点。
word_family.example_sentence: The effects were myriadfold and hard to measure.

related_words.note: 可作为理解 myriad 的熟词桥梁。
related_words.example_sentence: Countless insects gathered near the light.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 显露; 显示 unintentionally reveal; be evidence of: His accent betrayed his nationality. 从他的口音可知道他是哪国人 。 | His remark betrays his lack of concern. 他的话暴露出他冷漠的

headline.core_en: to be disloyal to someone; also to reveal something unintentionally

familiar_bridge.summary: betray 最熟的是“背叛”；在 GRE 阅读里也常表示“无意中暴露出”。

core.main: 本书重点是“显露”：口音、表情、话语会 betray 某种身份、情绪或态度。

usage_groups.group_title: 无意中显露身份、情绪或态度
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于 accent、remark、expression、tone 等泄露原本没明说的信息。

examples.sentence: His accent betrayed his nationality before he said where he was from.
examples.translation: 他还没说自己来自哪里，口音就暴露了他的国籍。
examples.teaching_note: 这里 betray 是“无意显露”。

examples.sentence: Her calm voice betrayed no sign of fear.
examples.translation: 她平静的声音没有露出任何恐惧。
examples.teaching_note: betray no sign of... 是自然表达。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 表示背弃朋友、国家、原则或承诺。

examples.sentence: He betrayed his colleagues by sharing their private emails.
examples.translation: 他泄露同事的私人邮件，背叛了他们。
examples.teaching_note: 这是更常见的“背叛”义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The data revealed a sharp decline in sales.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: reveal 可有意或无意；betray 常强调不由自主地暴露。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her smile showed relief.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: show 最普通；betray 带“泄露出隐藏信息”的感觉。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The official refused to divulge the source of the report.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: divulge 通常是有意透露秘密；betray 可是无意显露。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The company disclosed the risk in a public filing.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: disclose 比较正式，常指公开信息。

antonyms.example_sentence: He tried to conceal his disappointment.
antonyms.difference_note: conceal 是隐藏；betray 是显露。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Her cheerful tone belied her exhaustion.
confusable_words.difference_note: belie 常表示表面与真实不一致；betray 是让真实露出来。

word_family.example_sentence: The betrayal destroyed their friendship.

word_family.note: 做出 betray 行为的人。
word_family.example_sentence: The story never reveals the betrayer.

related_words.note: 与 betray 的背叛义相关。
related_words.example_sentence: The general was condemned as a traitor.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 诉讼 the process of taking legal action: be in litigation with sb. over sth. 就某事与某人进行诉讼

headline.core_en: the process of taking legal action in court

familiar_bridge.summary: legal action 是“法律行动”；litigation 指正式进入法院或法律程序的诉讼过程。

core.main: litigation 常用于公司、合同、专利、医疗等正式争端，不是普通 argument。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于公司、合同、知识产权、事故等进入法律程序。

examples.sentence: The two companies have been in litigation over the patent for years.
examples.translation: 两家公司围绕这项专利已经打了多年官司。
examples.teaching_note: be in litigation over sth 是原书结构。

examples.sentence: The hospital settled the case to avoid years of costly litigation.
examples.translation: 医院和解了此案，以避免多年昂贵的诉讼。
examples.teaching_note: costly litigation 强调诉讼耗时耗钱，常见于正式法律语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The workers filed a lawsuit against the factory.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: lawsuit 是具体案件；litigation 是诉讼过程或整体活动。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The group threatened legal action if the policy continued.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: legal action 更泛；litigation 更强调法院诉讼过程。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The prosecution presented new evidence.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: prosecution 多指刑事检控一方或过程；litigation 可指民事诉讼。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The contract requires arbitration before litigation.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: arbitration 是庭外仲裁；litigation 是法院诉讼。

antonyms.example_sentence: The parties reached a settlement before trial.

word_family.example_sentence: The company chose to litigate rather than settle.
Both litigants agreed to mediation.

related_words.note: litigation 属于 legal process。
related_words.example_sentence: The legal dispute lasted for years.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. SEEN; 影响广泛的 having important and widely applicable effects or implications: a far-reaching influence 深远的影响

headline.core_cn: 影响深远的；波及广泛的
headline.core_en: having important effects that extend widely or last for a long time

familiar_bridge.summary: far-reaching 字面像“影响伸得很远”，重点是后果范围大、时间长。

core.main: far-reaching 常修饰 effects、consequences、implications、reforms 等。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于政策、发现、改革、决定等产生长期或广泛影响。

examples.sentence: The court decision had far-reaching consequences for civil rights law.
examples.translation: 这项法院判决对民权法产生了深远影响。
examples.teaching_note: far-reaching consequences 是最自然的搭配。

examples.sentence: The discovery may have far-reaching implications for climate research.
examples.translation: 这一发现可能对气候研究产生深远影响。
examples.teaching_note: implications 强调后续意义和影响。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report raised an important question.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: important 只说明重要；far-reaching 强调影响范围和后果延伸。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The reform had wide-ranging effects on schools.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: wide-ranging 和 far-reaching 接近，但不一定强调深远后果。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The war had a profound effect on the country.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: profound 强调深度；far-reaching 强调范围广、影响延伸远。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The law introduced sweeping changes to the tax system.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: sweeping 常修饰改革或变化，强调全面。

antonyms.example_sentence: The policy had only a limited effect on prices.
antonyms.difference_note: limited 与 far-reaching 的广泛影响相反。

word_family.note: far-reaching 的核心来自 reach 的“伸展到”。
word_family.example_sentence: The river reaches the sea.
The far-reachingness of the change became clear later.

related_words.note: 同在“影响范围广”的语义场。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.i. 384; 集中 be or come into a cluster or close group; congregate: The girls clustered around their teacher, 女学生们聚集在教师的周围 。 n. #; 团; 群 agroup of similar things or people positioned or occurring closely together: a cluster of flowers — #& #4¢, | a cluster of antique shops 一推古董店

headline.core_en: a close group of similar things or people; to gather closely together

familiar_bridge.summary: cluster 像一小团东西靠在一起：花、商店、人群、数据点都可以形成 cluster。

core.main: cluster 既可作名词“群、簇”，也可作动词“聚集”。

usage_groups.group_title: 相似事物聚成一群或一簇
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调多个相似的人或物彼此靠近，形成一小团或一片。

examples.sentence: A cluster of antique shops lines the narrow street near the station.
examples.translation: 车站附近那条窄街上聚集着一片古董店。
examples.teaching_note: a cluster of shops 是原书语境的自然表达。

examples.sentence: The telescope revealed a cluster of stars near the edge of the galaxy.
examples.translation: 望远镜显示出银河边缘附近的一团恒星。
examples.teaching_note: cluster 可以用于自然科学语境。

usage_groups.group_title: 人围聚在某人或某物周围
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 作动词时，表示人或物靠拢聚集。

examples.sentence: The children clustered around their teacher to see the map.
examples.translation: 孩子们围在老师身边看地图。
examples.teaching_note: cluster around 是动词义的核心结构。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: A small group waited outside the office.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: group 很普通；cluster 强调靠得近、聚成一团。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: A crowd gathered in front of the theater.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: crowd 多指大量人；cluster 可指小群人或物。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Visitors congregated near the entrance after the lecture.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: congregate 更正式，主要用于人聚集。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The city grew into a dense agglomeration of villages.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: agglomeration 更学术，指聚合成的整体。

antonyms.example_sentence: The birds scattered when the dog ran toward them.
antonyms.difference_note: scatter 与 cluster 的聚集相反。

word_family.note: cluster 的过去分词形容用法。
word_family.example_sentence: The houses were clustered along the river.

word_family.note: 常见于数据分析或社会现象。
word_family.example_sentence: The study examined the clustering of disease cases.

related_words.note: 和 cluster 的空间画面相近。
related_words.example_sentence: A clump of trees stood beside the road.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 坏脾气的; 满腹牢骚的 irritable and bad-tempered; grumpy; complaining: a grouchy old man 坏脾气的老头

headline.core_en: irritable, bad-tempered, and likely to complain

familiar_bridge.summary: grouchy 是那种一不舒服、一疲惫就容易发牢骚的坏脾气。

core.main: grouchy 语气偏日常，常形容人、语气、反应一时或习惯性不耐烦。

usage_groups.group_title: 人因为疲惫、不舒服而脾气坏
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调人处在烦躁、易怒、爱抱怨的情绪状态。

examples.sentence: After the long flight, everyone was tired and grouchy.
examples.translation: 长途飞行后，大家都又累又烦躁。
examples.teaching_note: feel/be grouchy 很自然，表示一时脾气差。

examples.sentence: The grouchy old man complained about every noise in the building.
examples.translation: 那个坏脾气的老人抱怨楼里的每一点声音。
examples.teaching_note: a grouchy old man 是原书搭配。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The child was grumpy after missing his nap.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: grumpy 和 grouchy 很接近，常指暂时不高兴。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Lack of sleep made her irritable.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: irritable 更正式，强调容易被刺激而发怒。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The cantankerous neighbor argued with everyone on the street.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: cantankerous 更强，常指长期难相处。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His curmudgeonly comments amused the staff.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: curmudgeonly 更书面或幽默，常形容爱抱怨的人。

antonyms.example_sentence: She remained cheerful despite the delay.
antonyms.difference_note: cheerful 与 grouchy 的坏脾气相反。

word_family.note: grouchy 的相关名词和动词。
word_family.example_sentence: He is a grouch before breakfast.
His grouchiness disappeared after lunch.

related_words.note: 和 grouchy 的抱怨感相关。
related_words.example_sentence: Passengers began to grumble about the delay.

source.raw_ocr_text: Aid HEX adj. 表面上的; 看上去的; 貌似的 seeming real or true, but not necessarily so: his apparent lack of concern 他表面上的漠不关心

headline.core_en: seeming to be true, or easy to see; sometimes not actually true

familiar_bridge.summary: appear 是“看起来”；apparent 可以是“看上去如此”，也可以是“显而易见”。

core.main: apparent 有两层：一是表面看起来如此但未必真实，二是明显可见。

usage_groups.group_title: 表面看起来如此，未必真实
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调表面证据让某事看似成立，但真实情况仍可能不同。

examples.sentence: His apparent lack of concern hid deep anxiety about the result.
examples.translation: 他表面上的漠不关心掩盖了对结果的深深焦虑。
examples.teaching_note: apparent 在这里暗示表面与真实可能不同。

examples.sentence: The apparent agreement between the two studies disappeared after closer analysis.
examples.translation: 进一步分析后，这两项研究表面上的一致消失了。
examples.teaching_note: apparent agreement 表示看似一致。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于事实、原因、变化等很清楚地呈现出来。

examples.sentence: It soon became apparent that the experiment had failed.
examples.translation: 很快就明显看出实验失败了。
examples.teaching_note: become apparent 是“变得明显”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The seeming simplicity of the problem was misleading.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: seeming 更强调表象；apparent 可表象也可明显。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The error was obvious to every reader.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: obvious 更确定；apparent 有时保留“看起来如此”的不确定性。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The ostensible reason for the visit was trade.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: ostensible 更强调公开宣称的理由可能不是真因。

antonyms.example_sentence: The hidden cause of the crisis emerged later.
antonyms.difference_note: hidden 与 apparent 的可见或表面相对。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The rules are transparent and easy to follow.
confusable_words.difference_note: transparent 是清楚透明；apparent 是显得如此或明显。

word_family.note: apparent 来自 appear 的“显现/看起来”。
word_family.example_sentence: The answer appears simple at first.

word_family.note: 帮助理解 apparent 的表面感。
word_family.example_sentence: Appearances can be misleading.

word_family.note: 副词，常表示根据表面情况判断。
word_family.example_sentence: Apparently, the train has been delayed.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. Xf; 敌人 one's opponent in a contest, conflict, or dispute: a sea power and her continental adversaries 一个海上强国及其在大陆上的敌人 | adversaries in a war 一场战争中敌对的双方派生 adversarial /,dvo'seortol/ adj. 敌对的; 对立的; 反对的 involving or characterized by conflict or opposition: one's adversarial stance 某人的敌对态度 i X44) antagonistic

headline.core_en: an opponent in a contest, conflict, dispute, or war

familiar_bridge.summary: adversary 比 enemy 更正式，常指竞争、争论或战争中的对立一方。

core.main: adversary 可以是政治、法律、军事或辩论中的对手，不一定是私人仇人。

usage_groups.group_title: 冲突、竞赛或争论中的对手
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于 war、debate、lawsuit、election 等对抗场景。

examples.sentence: The senator treated every critic as a political adversary.
examples.translation: 这位参议员把每个批评者都当作政治对手。
examples.teaching_note: political adversary 是正式搭配。

examples.sentence: In court, each lawyer must understand the adversary’s strongest argument.
examples.translation: 在法庭上，每位律师都必须理解对手最强的论点。
examples.teaching_note: legal adversary 指诉讼或辩论中的对手。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She defeated her opponent in the final round.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: opponent 最普通；adversary 更正式、更有对抗感。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The army advanced into enemy territory.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: enemy 常指敌人或仇敌；adversary 可用于非战争争端。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The novel presents the brother as the main antagonist.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: antagonist 可指文学反派或对立力量；adversary 更常指现实对手。

antonyms.example_sentence: The two former rivals became allies after the election.
antonyms.difference_note: ally 是合作一方，与 adversary 相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The debate became increasingly adversarial.
confusable_words.difference_note: adversarial 是形容词；adversary 是人或团体。

word_family.note: 形容关系、态度或制度充满对抗。
word_family.example_sentence: The negotiations took an adversarial turn.

word_family.note: 同源于“逆着、相反”的方向感，但意思是困难处境。
word_family.example_sentence: She showed courage in adversity.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 机械般的; 没有思想的 mechanical: mechanistic policy 机械的政策

headline.core_cn: 机械式的；机械论的；缺少灵活性的
headline.core_en: machine-like, rigid, or explaining things as if they work by mechanical processes

familiar_bridge.summary: mechanism 是“机制”；mechanistic 像把复杂的人、社会或生命现象当机器来处理。

core.main: mechanistic 可批评政策或思维僵硬，也可在科学中表示“机制层面的解释”。

usage_groups.group_title: 政策、反应或思维显得僵硬机械
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 形容做法按固定程序运转，缺少判断、弹性或人味。

examples.sentence: The school’s mechanistic policy left teachers no room to respond to individual students.
examples.translation: 学校机械化的政策让教师没有空间回应个别学生的情况。
examples.teaching_note: mechanistic policy 来自这个用法，强调僵硬。

examples.sentence: A mechanistic response to grief can make the sufferer feel ignored.
examples.translation: 对悲伤作机械式回应会让痛苦中的人觉得被忽视。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调缺少人情和判断。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调解释某现象内部如何运转，重在因果步骤和机制。

examples.sentence: The paper offers a mechanistic explanation of how the drug affects memory.
examples.translation: 论文从机制层面解释了这种药物如何影响记忆。
examples.teaching_note: 科学语境中 mechanistic 不一定贬义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His apology sounded mechanical and insincere.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: mechanical 更普通；mechanistic 更常用于理论、政策或解释方式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: A rigid rule cannot handle unusual cases.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: rigid 强调不灵活；mechanistic 强调像机器般按程序运行。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The critic called the theory reductionist because it ignored culture.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: reductionist 指把复杂现象过度简化；mechanistic 可有类似风险。

antonyms.example_sentence: A flexible policy can adapt to local conditions.
antonyms.difference_note: flexible 与 mechanistic 的僵硬相反。

word_family.example_sentence: The mechanism of the disease remains unclear.
The task became mechanical after a few hours.

related_words.note: 帮助理解“像机器一样”的画面。
related_words.example_sentence: The machine sorted the letters automatically.

source.raw_ocr_text: #444) v.t. BHiE; 禁止 prevent or prohibit (someone) from doing something or from going somewhere: rush troops to bar the enemy's advance 和急调部队去阻挡敌军前进 i 044) preclude, prevent, rule out

headline.core_en: to prevent someone from doing something or entering somewhere; also a long narrow piece

familiar_bridge.summary: bar 作名词是“横条、栏杆”；作动词时像用横杆挡住，表示禁止或阻止。

core.main: 本词条重点是动词：bar someone from doing sth 或 bar an advance。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于法律、规则或机构阻止某人做某事。

examples.sentence: The new rule bars visitors from entering the archive without permission.
examples.translation: 新规定禁止访客未经许可进入档案馆。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

examples.sentence: Several athletes were barred from competition after the test results.
examples.translation: 检测结果出来后，几名运动员被禁止参赛。
examples.teaching_note: be barred from competition 很自然。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用于军队、道路、障碍物阻止某种推进。

examples.sentence: Troops were rushed to bar the enemy’s advance.
examples.translation: 部队被急调去阻挡敌军前进。
examples.teaching_note: bar the enemy’s advance 来自原书语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The warning prevented a serious accident.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: prevent 很普通；bar 更常有规则或障碍物阻挡的感觉。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The law prohibits smoking in public buildings.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: prohibit 更正式法律化；bar 可用于准入、参赛、前进等。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The lack of evidence precludes a firm conclusion.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: preclude 更书面，表示使某事不可能。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Doctors ruled out infection as the cause.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: rule out 强调排除可能性；bar 强调禁止或阻挡。

antonyms.example_sentence: The policy allows students to choose their courses.
antonyms.difference_note: allow 与 bar 的禁止相反。

word_family.note: bar 的过去分词，可作形容词。
word_family.example_sentence: He was barred from the meeting.

word_family.note: 和 bar 的阻挡感相关。
word_family.example_sentence: Language can become a barrier to access.

related_words.note: 也是一种正式禁止，但多用于贸易或运输。
related_words.example_sentence: The country imposed an embargo on weapons.

source.raw_ocr_text: prep. 除非有 …; 如果没有 … except for: if not for: She's going to lose the election barring a miracle, 她会输掉选举 ， 除非有奇迹

headline.core_en: except for; if something does not happen

familiar_bridge.summary: barring 来自 bar 的“排除”：把某个例外排除掉之后，事情通常会按预期发生。

core.main: barring 常放句首，表示“除非出现某种意外情况”。

usage_groups.group_title: 除非出现某个例外情况
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调在没有意外例外发生时，结果通常会按预期出现。

examples.sentence: She is going to lose the election, barring a miracle.
examples.translation: 除非出现奇迹，否则她会输掉选举。
examples.teaching_note: barring a miracle 是原书例句的结构。

examples.sentence: Barring unexpected delays, the museum will reopen in June.
examples.translation: 如果没有意外延误，博物馆将在六月重新开放。
examples.teaching_note: barring 放句首时很自然。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Except for one error, the report was accurate.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: except for 更普通；barring 更常用于预期和例外条件。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Unless it rains, the match will continue.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unless 接完整从句；barring 后通常接名词短语。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Save for a few objections, the plan was accepted.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: save for 更书面，和 barring 在“除去例外”上接近。

antonyms.example_sentence: The price is $50, including tax.
antonyms.difference_note: including 是纳入；barring 是排除例外。

word_family.note: barring 来自 bar 的“排除、挡开”方向。
word_family.example_sentence: The rule bars visitors from entering.
The player was barred from the tournament.
